title: The Cock and the Fox
lines: A Cock was sitting on a fence, crowing as loudly as he could.
A Fox heard the noise and came running.
"I have come to make peace with all the animals."
"From this day on, all birds and beasts shall be brothers."
The Cock was delighted at the news, but he noticed the Fox looking keenly down the road.
"What do you see?" asked the Cock.
"There are some hunters coming," said the Fox.
"But never mind, I have made peace with them too."
"Oh, that is good," said the Cock, and he flew into a high tree.
"Why do you fly?" asked the Fox.
"If you have made peace with the hunters, you may wait here," said the Cock, "but I prefer to be in a safe place."
moral: Fine promises are often made by those who cannot keep them.
